core_mechanism: deterministic time-integration of a Hamiltonian billiard flow — free ballistic motion under a velocity-dependent (Lorentz-type) force with specular reflection at hard boundaries — exhibiting a parameter-tuned mixed phase space in which islands of regular/quasiperiodic motion coexist with a chaotic sea
brief_reason: The abstract integrates the classical equations of motion of particles bouncing in a bounded geometry under a magnetic (Lorentz) force and reports regularity islands, quasiperiodic signatures, and dominant chaos that appear or vanish as control parameters (aspect ratio, field strength, interaction) are varied — the hallmark of a billiard's regular-to-chaotic transition in a mixed phase space.
